title: Research Quicklinks
description: Living links to the independent research sources Third i uses when building AI roadmaps. Each link explains what the source is and when it's useful.

links.title: Artificial Analysis
links.description: Independently measured model intelligence, performance, and cost across all major AI models.
links.whenToUse: When comparing which AI model to use for a specific task — speed, cost, and intelligence benchmarks side by side.

links.title: Arena Leaderboard
links.description: Human-preference rankings with accuracy ratings — real people vote on which AI model produces better output.
links.whenToUse: When you want to know which model humans actually prefer for writing, reasoning, or creative tasks — not just benchmark scores.

links.title: FutureTools
links.description: A directory of AI tools organized by category — discovery and category scanning for new capabilities.
links.whenToUse: When looking for a specific AI tool by use case (writing, video, coding, etc.). Popularity is not proof of suitability — use for discovery, not final selection.

links.title: Official Vendor Documentation
links.description: Official documentation and release notes from AI vendors (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.). Product capabilities, limits, pricing, and security.
links.whenToUse: When confirming what a specific AI product can actually do, its limits, pricing, and security posture — before making a recommendation.

links.title: Peer-Reviewed Research
links.description: Peer-reviewed and institutional research on AI adoption, risk, and impact evidence.
links.whenToUse: When evaluating the broader evidence base for an AI approach — adoption patterns, risk profiles, and measured impact from academic studies.

source: Third i research methodology — sources used when building AI roadmaps for clients
